model_narrative: We place knights on a square chessboard. For every cell we decide whether or not a knight sits there, and we track the total count of knights on the board. The objective is to maximize the number of knights placed.

sets.name: i
sets.doc: the board coordinates along one side, given as string labels of the integers 1 through 8; a cell on the board is an ordered pair drawn from this set used for both the row index and the column index

sets.name: n
sets.doc: the eight possible knight moves, each one of the L-shaped relative jumps a knight can make from a given cell

sets.name: axis
sets.doc: the two board axes along which a move is measured: h for the horizontal (row) direction and v for the vertical (column) direction

params.name: move
params.doc: the signed displacement of each knight move along each axis, in cells; for a move, adding its horizontal displacement to a cell's row and its vertical displacement to that cell's column gives the cell that move lands on, which may fall off the board

vars.name: x
vars.doc: the placement indicator for a cell, equal to 1 if a knight is placed on that cell and 0 otherwise; the first index is the row and the second is the column

vars.name: total
vars.doc: the total number of knights placed anywhere on the board

constraints.name: deftotal
constraints.intent: The total count of knights must match the placements actually made, so it equals the number of cells across the whole board that hold a knight.

constraints.name: move['h',
constraints.intent: No two knights may threaten one another. For every cell and every knight move, look at the cell reached by applying that move; if that target cell lies on the board, then at most one of the original cell and the target cell may hold a knight. When the target cell falls off the board the requirement does not apply.

query: We're evaluating a rule that keeps the top-left 2x2 corner block uncrowded — at most one knight across squares (1,1), (1,2), (2,1) and (2,2). Plug that in and tell me what the total knight count comes to.

query: Suppose we insist all four corner squares carry a knight — (1,1), (1,8), (8,1) and (8,8) all occupied. Run that and let me know how many knights the board can hold under that requirement.

query: We want to look at keeping the left half of the board lighter — no more than 14 knights across columns 1 through 4. What does the optimizer return for the overall count under that limit?
expected_pyomo: model.cap_left = Constraint(expr=sum(model.x[i, j] for i in model.i for j in ['1', '2', '3', '4']) <= 14)

query: The whole of column 1 is sitting half-used. Why not just leave that entire leftmost file empty and pack the knights elsewhere — surely we'd still seat the same number? What's the model seeing that makes vacating column 1 cost us?
expected_pyomo: model.empty_col1 = Constraint(expr=sum(model.x[i, '1'] for i in model.i) == 0)
